_note: Reshaped for run_evals: every assertion carries _gating + a machine `check` where the scenario is deterministically runnable offline (no API key). `exec` runs from `cwd` with placeholders {py} {repo} {root} {out} {runtime_root}. Scenario `interactive-dry-run-preview` stays structural-without-check (UNMAPPED, reported manual): build_manifest --write path-guards to the repo root, so it cannot run in a sandbox tmp.

scenarios.description: Pack a minimal manifest into a versioned tar.gz with a reproducible SHA256.

assertions.text: second run produces a byte-identical tarball (identical SHA256)

scenarios.description: safety_check on a synthetic repo with .env + .git emits warn-level always-drop findings WITHOUT echoing file content. The dirty tree is generated at runtime (setup.files) because .env is gitignored and cannot be a committed fixture.

assertions.text: findings include an always_drop rule for exact:.env
findings include an always_drop rule for dir:.git
no .env file CONTENT echoed to stdout/stderr

scenarios.description: Interactive answers -> build_manifest --write -> pack --dry-run preview. NOT sandbox-runnable: build_manifest --write path-guards writes to the repo root, so a tmp --out is refused. Assertions kept as structural (no machine check) -> reported UNMAPPED (manual), never a silent pass.
scenarios._manual: Run by hand from release/scripts; see assertions below.

assertions.text: generated YAML starts with '# Generated by cleanmatic:release'
pack --dry-run does NOT create a tar.gz file
